name: Insulin and IGF Axis Modulation Coverage Assessment for June 3, 2026

assessment_window.basis: Coverage assessment created during ordinary surveillance after the published bootstrap baseline.

summary: Coverage is enough to support the current low-confidence human_biomarker_signal public claim, but it is still thin as a coverage map. The baseline represents rare human natural experiments, centenarian genetics, exceptional-longevity IGF-1 survival associations, FMD biomarker movement, animal lifespan biology, protein-intake age boundaries, and older-adult GH safety context. The surveillance pass found no post-publication public-update delta, but it surfaced pre-window acarbose aging and FMD/longevity-diet registry records that were not staged. Those registry records should be handled as coverage repair before another ordinary surveillance pass.

evidence_categories.rationale: The current support map includes FMD randomized biomarker movement and older-adult GH administration safety context, but no intentional insulin/GH/IGF-axis aging intervention shows functional, disease-incidence, disability, or mortality benefit. Acarbose human aging registry records were found during surveillance but have not yet been curated into public context.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Coverage repair should review NCT02953093 and NCT02865499 before treating acarbose as animal-only support in this track.

evidence_categories.rationale: The public baseline covers Ecuadorian GHRD natural-experiment evidence, IGF1R centenarian genetics, and exceptional-longevity IGF-1 survival associations with subgroup caveats.

The baseline includes classic and recent animal support across IGF1R heterozygosity, GHR disruption, adipocyte-specific GHR disruption, fat-specific insulin receptor knockout, IRS1 null mice, and acarbose HET3 lifespan evidence.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Animal coverage is adequate for the current claim but still heterogeneous by sex, tissue, timing, and intervention class.

evidence_categories.rationale: Mechanistic coverage includes GH/IGF deficiency, IGF1R signaling, insulin sensitivity, Akt/Foxo signaling, protein intake and IGF-1 interactions, and acarbose glucose-excursion context.

The support map explicitly includes age-dependent protein-intake boundaries, opposite-direction GH administration safety harms, subgroup limits in human IGF-1 survival evidence, and the non-interventional nature of rare genetic support.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Acarbose human registry results may add limiting or null context after source-fidelity review.

evidence_categories.rationale: Safety coverage is dominated by older-adult GH administration harms and broad endocrine tradeoff reasoning. There is no adequate preventive-use safety, durability, frailty, sarcopenia, bone, repair, or glucose-control evidence for an adult-onset axis-modulating gerotherapy.

Surveillance found acarbose aging registry records with posted results and FMD/longevity-diet registry records without posted results, but these have not yet been curated into source, activity, finding, or exclusion records.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Review NCT02953093, NCT02865499, NCT05698654, NCT07065955, and NCT07255300 during coverage repair.

evidence_categories.rationale: The bootstrap and surveillance passes checked targeted primary and registry sources, but no formal recent-review or citation-network audit has been recorded for this track.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Run a selective recent-review triangulation during coverage repair, especially for GH/IGF modulation, acarbose, protein restriction, and FMD.

evidence_categories.rationale: The baseline includes major local anchors for GHRD, IGF1R centenarian genetics, low IGF-1 survival, IGF1R mouse lifespan, GHRKO signaling, adult-onset and tissue-specific GHR mouse work, FIRKO, IRS1 null mice, acarbose, protein intake, and GH administration.
evidence_categories.gap_note: Adequate does not mean exhaustive; recent review triangulation is still missing.

evidence_categories.rationale: The public outlook keeps FMD and acarbose as cross-track support, separates GH administration as opposite-direction safety context, and avoids treating rare lifelong genetic evidence as an adult-onset therapy.

search_log_summary: The surveillance pass reviewed the published outlook, bootstrap session, bundle, evidence reviews, publication event, same-day PubMed searches for IGF/GH/insulin-axis aging terms, and ClinicalTrials.gov searches for acarbose and FMD/longevity-diet aging registries.
source_selection_notes: PMID 42233927 was not staged because tirzepatide obesity biomarker movement is adjacent cardiometabolic context, not a defined insulin/GH/IGF aging intervention for this track.
PMID 42233597 and PMID 42230695 were excluded as wrong-scope same-day PubMed hits.
NCT02953093 and NCT02865499 were not staged in surveillance because they predate the baseline and need source-fidelity review as acarbose coverage-repair candidates.
NCT07065955, NCT07255300, and NCT05698654 were retained as FMD/longevity-diet trial-watch candidates for coverage repair because they lack posted public results in this pass.

known_gaps.description: No controlled human intervention intentionally modulating insulin, GH, IGF-1, IGF1R, or a defined downstream node has shown aging-relevant functional, disease, disability, multimorbidity, or mortality benefit.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep the public stage at human_biomarker_signal unless future controlled human studies report functional or durable clinical outcomes with acceptable endocrine safety.

known_gaps.description: Acarbose aging registry records with posted results and FMD/longevity-diet registry records without posted results were identified during surveillance but are not represented in the public support map or as explicit exclusions.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Run coverage repair to stage or explicitly exclude NCT02953093, NCT02865499, NCT05698654, NCT07065955, and NCT07255300.

known_gaps.description: Adult-onset preventive endocrine modulation remains safety-sensitive because muscle, bone, repair, cancer, diabetes, frailty, and glucose-control tradeoffs are unresolved.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Require explicit safety, durability, tissue-selectivity, and responder-selection evidence before strengthening confidence.

known_gaps.description: No formal recent-review or citation-network audit is recorded for the insulin/IGF axis modulation track.
known_gaps.suggested_action: During coverage repair, check recent reviews for missing primary anchors and for limiting evidence around GH/IGF suppression, acarbose, protein restriction, and FMD.

known_gaps.description: FMD and acarbose are cross-track evidence shared with caloric-restriction mimetics and related nutrient-sensing tracks, so future updates could accidentally inflate the insulin/IGF claim by inheritance.
known_gaps.suggested_action: Keep future public changes explicit about whether evidence supports insulin/GH/IGF-axis modulation itself or only adjacent metabolic, CR-mimetic, or autophagy mechanisms.

next_coverage_action: Run a bounded coverage-repair pass focused on acarbose aging registries, FMD/longevity-diet trial-watch records, and recent review triangulation before ordinary surveillance resumes.
